cards.title: MIT: what agentic AI actually is (and isn't)

panels.head: "AI that takes actions in the world."
panels.sub: MIT's Phillip Isola cuts through the buzzword: an agent doesn't just answer — it does.

panels.head: 35% already deployed. 44% about to.
panels.sub: An MIT Sloan / BCG survey found over a third of businesses run AI agents today, with nearly half more planning to.

panels.head: From chatbot to coworker.
panels.sub: The difference isn't intelligence — it's permission. Agents get tools, credentials and the authority to act.

panels.head: Authority needs accountability.
panels.sub: The open question is oversight: who reviews what an agent did, and who owns the mistake?

panels.head: Same question FOMI answers for inspection.
panels.sub: Auditable per-action records are what turn autonomous systems into deployable ones.

cards.title: 5 habits that separate vibe coders from the mess
cards.headline: AI writes the code.

steps.label: Feed it a CLAUDE.md
steps.detail: Every session starts cold. A project memory file with your stack, patterns and no-go zones fixes that.

steps.label: Assign a role
steps.detail: "Act as a senior engineer burned by untested payment code" produces more defensive output than "build this".

steps.label: Build in layers
steps.detail: One prompt per layer, not one prompt for the whole integration. Tangled generations are where bugs hide.

steps.label: Review like a PR
steps.detail: A scan of 5,600 vibe-coded apps found 400+ exposed secrets. Read diffs like they came from a junior dev.

steps.label: List the off-limits
steps.detail: Auth, secrets and billing logic go in CLAUDE.md as explicitly untouchable — and get repeated in risky prompts.

cards.title: Plan Mode: the 20 seconds that save an hour
cards.caption: Plan first, approve, then build. Slower for the first minute, faster for the whole session.

script.text: > Enter Plan Mode: add offline caching to the feed app
Exploring codebase… reading service worker setup…
PLAN: 1) cache feed.json on fetch  2) stale-while-
revalidate strategy  3) fallback card when offline

cards.title: 3 CV papers worth 60 seconds this month
cards.headline: MIT's vision lab,

steps.label: Spatial memory for robots
steps.detail: A system that efficiently remembers object details while a robot explores — persistent scene understanding, not frame-by-frame.

steps.label: Sonar-MASt3R
steps.detail: Fuses sonar with visual data for real-time 3D maps in cloudy water. The pattern — fuse a second modality where optics fail — travels well.

steps.label: ChartNet dataset
steps.detail: Training data aimed at making vision-language models actually read charts and figures correctly.

cards.title: Coding agents come to edge vision dev

panels.head: The agents are coming for embedded dev.
panels.sub: This week's Edge AI & Vision Alliance digest centres on edge-first coding agents that target real devices.

panels.head: Edge dev punishes iteration.
panels.sub: Cross-compiling, flashing hardware, camera quirks — the feedback loop that agents shortcut on the web barely exists here.

panels.head: Agents that build for the device, not the browser.
panels.sub: Toolchains that let an agent compile, deploy and test against actual edge hardware close that loop.

panels.head: Watch this for Jetson workflows.
panels.sub: If agent-assisted edge dev matures, small teams shipping camera systems get disproportionately faster.

panels.head: The bottleneck moved.
panels.sub: Industry coverage this month argues the constraint is no longer raw compute — it's orchestrating and governing fleets of agents.

panels.head: Acquisitions and donations.
panels.sub: Palo Alto acquired Portkey; Solo.io handed its agentgateway project to the Linux Foundation. Consolidation means a category is forming.

panels.head: One choke point for every agent action.
panels.sub: Policy, cost attribution, credentials and audit for agent traffic — the same job API gateways did for microservices.

panels.head: Governance is where the money went.
panels.sub: When security vendors buy the plumbing, enterprises are past the pilot stage.

panels.head: Agents are getting long-term memory.
panels.sub: AgentPrizm launched AgentMemory on July 9 — a REST + MCP layer giving agents persistent memory across sessions.

panels.head: Trust in what an agent remembers.
panels.sub: Persistent memory is easy to bolt on and hard to trust: what got stored, what got reused, what should be forgotten?

panels.head: An audit trail per memory decision.
panels.sub: Each remember/forget action can be shown back to the user, with right-to-forget controls built in.

panels.head: Auditability is the product now.
panels.sub: Across agents, memory and gateways, the same pattern: the feature enterprises pay for is the paper trail.
